mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below 52% in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 248.0 indicates persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 248.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of historical cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, systematically generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5% signals supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances despite the industrial production drop, or the signal predictive power disappears when FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%.
